title: Poll: Most Americans believe Supreme Court avoids ruling against Trump
shortDescription: A new poll shows most Americans believe the Supreme Court avoids ruling against Donald Trump, despite a recent ruling against Trump's tariffs.

title: Virginia redistricting: Judge blocks maps passed by voters
shortDescription: A Virginia judge blocked the certification of the vote approving a congressional map that would give Democrats a large majority in the state's delegation.

title: House Democrats press FBI chief Kash Patel over drinking claims
shortDescription: Democrats on the House Judiciary Committee sent FBI Director Kash Patel a letter, demanding that he submit to an alcohol abuse screener.

title: Trump Navy secretary John C. Phelan resigns
shortDescription: Navy Secretary John C. Phelan resigned as the Pentagon named Hung Cao to replace him amid tensions over the Iran naval blockade.

title: David Jackson, longtime White House correspondent for USA TODAY, dies
shortDescription: Jackson covered every president from Bill Clinton to Donald Trump, and was a fixture on the campaign trail.

title: Few Americans consider President Trump as religious, survey finds
shortDescription: President Trump recently clashed with the pope. Even before that, many U.S. adults did not consider him too religious, if at all, a survey finds.

title: White House not setting deadline for Iran talks
shortDescription: The White House declined to say how long President Trump is willing to wait for Iranian negotiators to respond as peace talks stall.

title: Senate once again votes down War Powers resolution
shortDescription: The Senate voted down a resolution to end American hostilities in Iran. It was once again a largely party line vote.

title: Senate candidate says he'd push to impeach 2 Supreme Court justices
shortDescription: Maine Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner told NBC News he would seek an investigation on President Donald Trump if he's elected to office.

title: Ending the DHS shutdown is getting tricky in Congress
shortDescription: Ending the Department of Homeland Security shutdown is about to get more complicated.

title: Trump claims Virginia redistricting vote was 'rigged' without evidence
shortDescription: The referendum eked out a victory by three percentage points that temporarily changes the state's boundaries for its delegation to the U.S. House.

title: Arrests at Earth Day protest outside Trump Tower
shortDescription: Climate activists demonstrating outside Trump Tower in honor of Earth Day were arrested after their sit-in blocked traffic on Fifth Avenue.

title: Does Tucker Carlson still support Donald Trump? Their fallout, explained
shortDescription: Conservative commentator Tucker Carlson has long supported Donald Trump. But after the war in Iran, he says he will 'be tormented by it for a long time.'

title: Trump administration faces lawsuit over sugary food ban in SNAP
shortDescription: The Trump administration is being sued over USDA waivers allowing five states to restrict SNAP benefits from buying sugary foods.

title: Bill Clinton crashes Cowboys news conference; Jerry Jones stumbles
shortDescription: Former President Bill Clinton made a surprise appearance at a Dallas Cowboys press conference, greeting Jerry Jones and joking about the NFL draft.

title: Gold Trump statue is being held 'hostage.' See why
shortDescription: Sculptor Alan Cottrill is keeping a bronze statue of President Donald Trump in an undisclosed location until he gets his full payment.

title: Contract for $400M White House ballroom keeps many donors secret
shortDescription: The contract for Trump's $400 million White House ballroom project remained secret until Public Citizen won its release in federal court.

title: Trump depicted himself as Jesus, slammed Pope. Then, a Bible reading.
shortDescription: President Trump has frustrated some supporters in recent weeks after sharing an image that depicted himself as Christ-like and then slammed the pope.

title: Journalists urge protest of Trump at correspondents dinner
shortDescription: A coalition of 200-plus journalists and press freedom groups urged the White House Correspondents' dinner organizers to call out Trump at the event.

title: Why social media is upset about the UGA women's tennis White House visit
shortDescription: The UGA women's tennis team are national champions, but during a recent visit to the White House, they were posed behind men, sparking outcry.

title: Trump's Bible reading is creating a stir. Why it's dividing
shortDescription: President Donald Trump read 2 Chronicles 7:11-22, Old Testament scripture, during a pre-recorded broadcast for America Reads the Bible on April 21.

title: Grindr hosting party ahead of White House dinner
shortDescription: Grindr, the LGBTQ dating app, is hosting its first-ever party ahead of the annual White House Correspondents Association Dinner.

title: Sculptor says golden Trump statue won’t be released unless he's paid
shortDescription: Ohio-based sculptor Alan Cottrill finished a 15-foot bronze statue of Donald Trump. But a fight over payment is getting international attention.
